definition: Commodity-exporting EM labor markets exhibit procyclical dynamics that amplify terms-of-trade shocks into macro crises. The labor market is the amplification mechanism — not just the commodity price but wage and fiscal rigidity makes reversals painful.
evidence: Chile: copper → wage growth → REER appreciation → 38% USD/CLP reversal
Colombia and Nigeria oil-driven episodes documented
pair_2 called this 'one of the most robust empirical regularities in EM macro'
Extensive historical evidence across multiple cycles and countries
open_questions: Can EM fiscal frameworks (sovereign wealth funds, fiscal rules) mitigate the procyclical amplification?
How does the nonlinear political risk from labor stress interact with the economic channel?
